name: US Great Plains Wheat Belt (Kansas)
capacity: ~7% of global wheat exports (hard red winter)
note: Dominant hard red winter wheat zone; risk: drought (La Nina), freeze-thaw heaving in spring

name: Canadian Prairies Wheat Belt (Saskatchewan)
note: Spring wheat and durum hub; risk: early frost, periodic drought on open prairie

name: Krasnodar / Rostov Wheat Belt
note: Largest single exporting nation; risk: spring drought, frost, geopolitical Black Sea disruption

name: Ukraine Winter Wheat Belt
note: High-yield black-soil steppe; risk: ongoing conflict, Odesa port blockade

name: Beauce Plain (France)
capacity: ~5% of global wheat exports; EU's largest wheat region
note: Soft wheat for milling and feed; risk: late spring drought, summer heat waves

name: Western Australia Wheatbelt
capacity: ~10% of global wheat exports in good years
note: Southern hemisphere counter-seasonal supply; risk: Indian Ocean Dipole drought, salinity

name: Argentine Pampas Wheat Belt
note: Southern hemisphere supplier; risk: La Nina drought, export taxes (retenciones)

name: India Punjab / Haryana Wheat Belt
note: Green Revolution heartland; risk: heat stress at grain-fill, groundwater depletion

name: US Corn Belt (Iowa / Illinois)
capacity: ~30% of global corn production; ~35% of exports
note: World's largest corn production complex; risk: summer drought, derecho storms

name: Mato Grosso Safrinha Corn Belt
region: Mato Grosso / Mato Grosso do Sul / Parana
capacity: ~14% of global corn exports; second crop after soy
note: Double-cropping safrinha system drives cost competitiveness; risk: delayed soy harvest compresses planting window

name: Argentina Pampas Corn Belt
note: Major supplier Apr-Jun; risk: La Nina reduces yields by 20-30% in severe years

name: Ukraine Corn Belt (Black Soil zone)
note: Deep chernozem soils; risk: conflict damage to storage, port access

name: Northeast China Corn Belt
note: China's corn basket feeds massive livestock sector; risk: spring flooding, early frost

name: Mato Grosso Soybean Belt
capacity: ~20% of global soybean production; Brazil total ~38%
note: Cerrado frontier expansion; risk: deforestation pressure, El Nino drought, logistics

name: US Soybean Belt (Iowa / Illinois)
capacity: ~28% of global soybean production; ~30% of exports
note: Rotation with corn; risk: summer drought, trade policy (China tariffs)

name: Argentine Pampas Soybean Belt (Rosario hub)
capacity: ~14% of global soy exports as meal+oil+beans
note: Largest soy processing cluster globally (Rosario crush corridor); risk: La Nina, export taxes

name: Paraguay Eastern Soybean Belt
note: Landlocked; exports via Parana river or Brazil ports; risk: river drought cuts barge transport

name: India Punjab / Haryana Rice Belt
note: Basmati rice premium export; risk: groundwater depletion, heat at flowering

name: India West Bengal / Eastern Plains Rice Belt
note: Multiple crops per year; risk: Bay of Bengal cyclones, flooding

name: Vietnam Mekong Delta Rice Belt
note: Three-crop system; risk: saltwater intrusion from Mekong low-flow years, upstream dams

name: Thailand Central Plains Rice Belt
note: Fragrant Hom Mali rice price premium; risk: El Nino drought reduces 2nd crop

name: China Hunan / Yangtze Basin Rice Belt
note: Double-rice heartland; risk: summer flooding (Yangtze), heavy metal soil contamination

name: Minas Gerais Coffee Belt (Brazil)
region: Minas Gerais / Sao Paulo / Espirito Santo
note: World's dominant producer; risk: biennial cycle, frost (July), drought

name: Vietnam Dak Lak Coffee Plateau
capacity: ~18% of global coffee production; #1 robusta exporter
note: Cheap robusta for instant coffee; risk: El Nino dry season stress

name: Colombia Coffee Belt (Eje Cafetero)
capacity: ~8% of global coffee production; mild washed arabica
note: Year-round harvest due to altitude; risk: La Nina excess rain causes leaf rust

name: Ethiopia Sidamo / Yirgacheffe Coffee Belt
capacity: ~5% of global coffee production; birthplace of arabica
note: Heirloom varieties command specialty premiums; risk: drought, political instability

name: Cote d'Ivoire Cocoa Belt
note: Single-country dominance; risk: swollen shoot virus, black pod disease, EUDR rules

name: Ghana Cocoa Belt (Ashanti / Brong-Ahafo)
capacity: ~13% of global cocoa production; premium quality
note: COCOBOD quality control supports premium; risk: Harmattan dry spells, aging tree stock

name: Ecuador Cocoa Belt (Los Rios / Guayas)
capacity: ~6% of global cocoa exports; #1 in fine-flavor
note: Unique fine-flavor Nacional variety; risk: El Nino flooding, frosty pod rot

name: Nigeria Cocoa Belt (Southwest)
note: 4th largest producer but declining investment; risk: poor post-harvest infrastructure

name: Brazil Sao Paulo Center-South Sugarcane Belt
note: Flex-fuel mills pivot between sugar and ethanol; risk: frost (rare), drought

name: India Uttar Pradesh Sugarcane Belt
capacity: ~17% of global sugar production; periodic export surplus
note: State-controlled cane price (SAP) distorts output; risk: monsoon failure

name: Thailand Northeast Sugarcane Belt
capacity: ~7% of global sugar exports (3rd largest exporter)
note: Rainy season crop; risk: El Nino drought sharply cuts cane yield and sucrose

name: Sumatra Palm Oil Belt
capacity: ~30% of global palm oil production (Sumatra alone)
note: Indonesia ~55% of world supply total; risk: peatland fire/haze, EUDR rules

name: Kalimantan Palm Oil Belt
note: Frontier expansion into carbon-rich peatlands; risk: EUDR compliance, fire season

name: Sabah / Sarawak Palm Oil Belt (Malaysia)
capacity: ~27% of global palm oil exports; Malaysia #2 producer
note: Premium GHG-certified supply emerging; risk: labor shortage, EUDR supply chain rules

name: Xinjiang Cotton Belt
capacity: ~22% of global cotton production; ~85% of China's output
note: Machine-harvested long-staple; risk: sanctions (UFLPA), water scarcity (Tarim)

name: India Deccan Cotton Belt
capacity: ~23% of global cotton production (largest by area)
note: Smallholder Bt cotton dominant; risk: pink bollworm resistance, erratic monsoon

name: Texas High Plains Cotton
note: Dryland and irrigated Upland cotton; risk: Ogallala aquifer depletion, drought, hail

name: Brazil Cerrado Cotton Belt (Mato Grosso)
capacity: ~7% of global cotton exports; fastest-growing exporter
note: Second crop after soy; high-tech mechanized; risk: logistics distance to ports

name: Thailand Southern Rubber Belt
note: World's largest producer; risk: drought, falling prices vs synthetic, aging trees

name: Indonesia Sumatra / Kalimantan Rubber Belt
note: Smallholder dominated; risk: competition with palm oil for land

name: Vietnam Southeast Rubber Plantations
region: Binh Phuoc / Dak Lak / Tay Ninh
note: State-led expansion; also rubber in Laos/Cambodia via VRG; risk: dry season stress

name: Saskatchewan Potash Basin
capacity: ~32% of global potash production; largest reserves
note: World's largest mineable potash reserve; risk: mine flooding, rail logistics

name: Soligorsk Potash Mines (Belarus)
note: Sanctions-hit since 2021; rerouted via Russian ports; risk: sanctions, mine subsidence

name: Berezniki / Solikamsk Potash (Ural)
note: Rich Verkhnekamsk deposit; risk: sinkhole events, sanctions reducing export routes

name: Dead Sea Potash Works (Israel)
capacity: ~7% of global potash via solar evaporation
note: Solar evaporation ponds unique; Dead Sea shrinking raises long-term brine risk

name: Khouribga Phosphate Belt (OCP Morocco)
capacity: ~14% of phosphate rock; ~35% of global reserves
note: Morocco controls ~70% of world's economic reserves; OCP is global price-setter

name: Florida Phosphate District
note: Largest non-Moroccan single district; risk: reclamation costs, phosphogypsum stacks

name: Yunnan / Guizhou Phosphate Basin
note: China restricts exports to protect domestic fertilizer supply; risk: ore grade decline

name: US Gulf Coast Ammonia / Nitrogen Hub
note: Henry Hub gas price sets cost floor; risk: hurricane disruption, gas price spikes

name: Trinidad Ammonia Complex (Point Lisas)
note: Atlantic Basin's key ammonia hub; risk: mature gas fields declining

name: Qatar Ammonia / Urea Complex (Mesaieed)
note: North Field gas underpins ultra-low feedstock cost; risk: Gulf tensions, Hormuz

name: Russia Ammonia Belt (Volga)
note: Togliatti-Odesa ammonia pipeline severed by war; risk: sanctions, gas price linkage

name: Port of Santos (agro export)
capacity: ~28% of global soybean exports via Santos+Paranagua
note: Latin America's largest port; also corn, sugar; risk: BR-163 bottleneck, draft limit

name: US Gulf Export Complex (New Orleans)
note: World's busiest grain export corridor; risk: Mississippi low water, hurricanes

name: Rosario / Up-River Port Complex
note: World's largest soy crush corridor; risk: Parana waterway drought, export tax policy

name: Novorossiysk Grain Terminal
note: Primary Russian wheat export terminal; risk: Black Sea naval incidents, storm queues

name: Odesa / Chornomorsk Port Complex
capacity: ~12% of global wheat + corn exports pre-war
note: Critical hub operating via grain-corridor workarounds; risk: missile strikes, naval mines

name: Port of Paranagua
note: Key complement to Santos; risk: Serra do Mar road disruption, harvest congestion

name: Russia Volga / Ural Wheat Belt
note: High interannual yield variability; risk: dry continental climate, late spring frost

name: Egypt Nile Delta Wheat Belt
note: High domestic demand far exceeds production; risk: Nile water (GERD), heat at grain fill

name: China North China Plain Wheat Belt
note: Winter wheat zone; state reserve replenishment sets price floor; risk: aquifer depletion

name: Jordan Phosphate Mines
note: Key supplier to India; exports via Aqaba; risk: water scarcity, regional geopolitics

name: Kola Peninsula Apatite (Russia)
capacity: ~7% of global phosphate rock; low-cadmium ore
note: EU favors Kola over Moroccan ore for low Cd; risk: sanctions limiting PhosAgro exports

name: Kandla / Mundra Port (India Agro Gateway)
capacity: ~4% of global wheat exports when India permits
note: Primary west-coast gateway for wheat/cotton exports; risk: India export ban policy

name: Pacific Northwest Wheat Export Hub
note: Gateway to Asian markets; risk: Columbia River barge low water

name: Kazakhstan Tselina Wheat Belt (Kostanay / Akmola)
note: Northern Kazakhstan's Virgin Lands steppe is the country's main spring wheat zone and a key Central Asian exporter.

name: Stavropol Krai Wheat Belt
note: Stavropol Krai is one of Russia's top three wheat-producing regions feeding Black Sea exports.

name: Western Siberia Wheat Belt (Altai / Omsk)
note: The Altai-Omsk steppe produces high-protein spring wheat supplying Siberia and Central Asian markets.

name: Northern Plains Spring Wheat Belt (North Dakota / Montana)
capacity: ~13 Mt hard red spring wheat / yr
note: North Dakota and Montana grow the bulk of US hard red spring and durum wheat for milling and export.

name: Alberta Wheat Belt (Southern Alberta)
note: Southern Alberta is the second-largest Canadian wheat province after Saskatchewan within the Prairie export complex.

name: New South Wales Wheat Belt (Riverina / Northern Plains)
note: New South Wales and its Riverina region are a major eastern Australian wheat zone shipping through east-coast ports.

name: Henan Winter Wheat Belt
note: Henan is China's leading wheat province, the core of the Huang-Huai-Hai winter wheat region.

name: Shandong Winter Wheat Belt
note: Shandong is China's second-largest wheat province within the irrigated North China Plain.

name: Madhya Pradesh Wheat Belt (Malwa Plateau)
note: Madhya Pradesh produces premium sharbati wheat and is a fast-growing pillar of India's wheat output.

name: Uttar Pradesh Wheat Belt (Gangetic Plain)
note: Uttar Pradesh is India's largest wheat-producing state across the fertile Gangetic Plain.

name: Cordoba Wheat Belt (Argentina)
note: Cordoba province anchors Argentina's northern wheat output feeding the Rosario up-river export complex.

name: Magdeburger Borde Wheat Belt (Germany)
note: The fertile Magdeburger Borde loess plain is Germany's prime soft wheat zone within the EU's largest wheat producer.

name: Hauts-de-France Wheat Belt (Picardy / Flanders)
note: The northern Hauts-de-France plains are among the EU's highest-yielding soft wheat areas supplying Rouen exports.

name: Greater Poland Wheat Belt (Wielkopolska)
note: Wielkopolska is a core region of Poland, the EU's third-largest wheat producer.

name: Konya Plain Wheat Belt (Anatolia)
note: The Konya Plain is the granary of Turkey, a leading global flour exporter and importer of milling wheat.

name: Pakistan Punjab Wheat Belt (Indus Plain)
note: Irrigated Punjab on the Indus plain is Pakistan's wheat heartland and a major domestic staple base.

name: Port of Rouen (Soft Wheat Export Hub)
note: Rouen on the Seine is Europe's largest grain export port, shipping French soft wheat to North Africa.

name: Rostov-on-Don / Azov Grain Terminals
note: Rostov and Azov are Russia's main shallow-draft river-sea ports loading wheat from the Don and Volga basins.

name: Port of Aktau (Caspian Grain Terminal)
note: Aktau on the Caspian is Kazakhstan's seaborne outlet for wheat exports toward Iran and the Caucasus.

name: Port of Constanta (Black Sea Grain Hub)
note: Constanta is the EU's largest Black Sea grain port, handling Romanian, Ukrainian and regional wheat and corn.

name: Eastern Corn Belt (Indiana / Ohio)
note: Indiana and Ohio form the eastern wing of the US Corn Belt feeding river and lake export logistics.

name: Nebraska Irrigated Corn Belt
note: Nebraska's irrigated Platte and Republican valleys make it the third-largest US corn-producing state.

name: Minnesota Corn Belt
note: Minnesota's southern counties are among the top US corn states, channeling grain via the Upper Mississippi.

name: Parana Summer Corn Belt (Brazil)
note: Parana is Brazil's leading first-crop (summer) corn state, complementing the center-west safrinha.

name: Goias Safrinha Corn Belt (Brazil)
note: Goias is a major center-west safrinha corn producer growing alongside soybeans on the cerrado.

name: Shandong Corn Belt (Huang-Huai-Hai)
note: Shandong anchors the Huang-Huai-Hai summer corn region, a core of China's domestic feed grain supply.

name: Jilin Corn Belt (Golden Corn Belt)
note: Jilin's black-soil plain is China's highest-yielding 'Golden Corn Belt' on the same latitude as the US belt.

name: Heilongjiang Corn Belt (Songnen Plain)
note: Heilongjiang is China's largest grain province, leading national corn output on the Songnen Plain.

name: India Bihar / Eastern Corn Belt
note: Bihar leads India's expanding rabi (winter) corn cultivation feeding poultry and starch demand.

name: India Karnataka Corn Belt (Deccan)
note: Karnataka is India's largest corn state, supplying the southern poultry feed and starch industries.

name: South Africa Maize Triangle (Free State / North West)
note: The Free State and North West Highveld form South Africa's Maize Triangle, the main sub-Saharan maize surplus zone.

name: Southwest France Corn Belt (Aquitaine)
note: Irrigated southwest France around the Adour valley is the EU's largest grain corn producing region.

name: Baragan Plain Corn Belt (Romania)
note: The Baragan and lower Danube plains make Romania one of the EU's top corn producers and exporters.

name: Sinaloa Corn Belt (Mexico)
note: Irrigated Sinaloa is Mexico's leading commercial white-corn state for the domestic tortilla supply.

name: Cordoba Corn Belt (Argentina)
note: Cordoba is Argentina's top corn province, feeding the Rosario up-river export terminals.

name: Left-Bank Ukraine Corn Belt (Poltava / Cherkasy)
note: The Poltava and Cherkasy forest-steppe is a high-yield Ukrainian corn zone shipping via Danube and Black Sea.

name: Lower Mississippi CGB Grain Terminals (Convent / Reserve)
note: The lower Mississippi terminals above New Orleans load the majority of US corn and soybean exports.

name: Port of Veracruz (Corn Import Gateway)
note: Veracruz is Mexico's principal Gulf port for importing US yellow corn for the livestock feed sector.

name: Parana Soybean Belt
note: Parana is Brazil's second-largest soybean state, centered on the fertile red-soil plateau around Cascavel and Ponta Grossa.

name: Rio Grande do Sul Soybean Belt
note: Rio Grande do Sul is a major southern soybean producer prone to La Nina drought stress around Passo Fundo and Cruz Alta.

name: Goias Soybean Belt
note: Goias in the central Cerrado is a fast-growing soybean frontier around Rio Verde and Jatai.

name: MATOPIBA Soybean Frontier (Bahia/Piaui)
note: MATOPIBA is Brazil's newest soybean frontier spanning western Bahia, southern Piaui, Tocantins and Maranhao on cleared Cerrado.

name: Minnesota Soybean Belt
note: Minnesota ranks among the top US soybean states, concentrated in the southern and southwestern counties.

name: Indiana/Ohio Soybean Belt
note: Indiana and western Ohio form the eastern Corn Belt with large rotational soybean acreage feeding river and rail terminals.

name: Nebraska Soybean Belt
note: Nebraska is a leading soybean state where center-pivot irrigation supports yields across the Platte valley.

name: North/South Dakota Soybean Belt
note: The Dakotas have expanded rapidly into soybeans, exporting much of the crop west via the Pacific Northwest rail corridor.

name: Cordoba Soybean Belt
note: Cordoba is Argentina's top soybean province on the western Pampas, trucking grain to the Rosario crushing cluster.

name: Alto Parana / Canindeyu Soybean Zone
note: Paraguay's eastern departments of Alto Parana and Canindeyu host intensive soybean cultivation barged down the Parana river.

name: Santa Cruz Soybean Belt
note: Santa Cruz lowlands account for nearly all of Bolivia's soybean output, much of it processed locally into meal and oil.

name: Ontario Soybean Belt
note: Southwestern Ontario is Canada's main soybean region, supplying food-grade beans for Asian export markets.

name: Heilongjiang Soybean Belt
note: Heilongjiang in the northeast produces most of China's domestic non-GM soybeans on the black-soil Sanjiang Plain.

name: Madhya Pradesh Soybean Belt
note: The Malwa Plateau of Madhya Pradesh is India's soybean heartland, supplying the domestic oilseed crushing industry.

name: Andhra Pradesh / Godavari Rice Belt
note: The Godavari and Krishna deltas of coastal Andhra Pradesh form one of India's most productive double-cropped rice regions.

name: Tamil Nadu / Cauvery Delta Rice Belt
note: The Cauvery delta around Thanjavur is the historic rice bowl of Tamil Nadu, irrigated by the Mettur reservoir system.

name: Uttar Pradesh Rice Belt
note: The Ganga plain of Uttar Pradesh is among India's largest rice-producing zones, grown in rotation with wheat.

name: Jiangsu / Lower Yangtze Rice Belt
note: Jiangsu on the lower Yangtze is a high-yield japonica rice region centered on the canal-laced Taihu plain.

name: Heilongjiang Rice Belt
note: Heilongjiang's Sanjiang Plain has become China's single largest rice-producing province on reclaimed cold-region paddies.

name: Northeast Thailand (Isan) Jasmine Rice Belt
note: The Isan plateau around Surin and Roi Et is the home of Thailand's premium Hom Mali jasmine rice for export.

name: Red River Delta Rice Belt
note: The densely farmed Red River delta around Hanoi is northern Vietnam's principal rice-growing region.

name: Java Rice Belt
note: The volcanic sawah terraces of Java produce the majority of Indonesia's rice, mainly in West and Central Java.

name: Bangladesh Ganges-Brahmaputra Rice Belt
note: Bangladesh grows three rice crops a year (aus, aman, boro) across the Ganges-Brahmaputra floodplain, ranking among the top global producers.

name: Pakistan Punjab / Sindh Rice Belt
note: The Kalar tract of Punjab and irrigated Sindh produce Pakistan's basmati and IRRI rice, a major export earner.

name: Ayeyarwady Delta Rice Belt
note: The Ayeyarwady (Irrawaddy) delta is Myanmar's rice bowl and the historic granary of mainland Southeast Asia.

name: Central Luzon Rice Belt
note: Central Luzon is the rice granary of the Philippines, irrigated by the Pampanga and Agno river systems.

name: Tonle Sap Rice Belt
note: The flood-recession plains around Cambodia's Tonle Sap lake produce aromatic rice now exported across Asia and the EU.

name: Arkansas Grand Prairie Rice Belt
note: Eastern Arkansas grows roughly half of all US rice on the Grand Prairie and Mississippi delta lowlands.

name: Sul de Minas Arabica Belt
note: Sul de Minas around Guaxupe is Brazil's largest arabica-producing zone and home to the world's biggest coffee cooperative, Cooxupe.

name: Cerrado Mineiro Coffee Region
note: Cerrado Mineiro near Patrocinio is a denomination-of-origin arabica zone defined by highly mechanized large estates on flat highland savanna.

name: Espirito Santo Conilon Belt
note: Espirito Santo is Brazil's dominant conilon (robusta) state, producing most of the country's robusta supply.

name: Lam Dong Highlands Coffee Belt
note: Lam Dong around Da Lat is Vietnam's second-largest coffee province and the heart of its emerging arabica production.

name: Gia Lai Robusta Belt
note: Gia Lai in Vietnam's Central Highlands is a major robusta-growing province feeding the country's export machine.

name: Huila Coffee Belt
capacity: ~2.5 million 60kg bags washed arabica per year
note: Huila is Colombia's single largest coffee-producing department, known for high-altitude washed arabica.

name: Gayo Highlands Coffee Belt (Aceh)
note: The Gayo highlands of Aceh are the largest arabica-producing area in Sumatra, famed for wet-hulled Mandheling-style coffee.

name: Lampung & South Sumatra Robusta Belt
note: Lampung and South Sumatra form Indonesia's main robusta belt, the bulk of the country's coffee exports through Panjang port.

name: Honduras Western Highlands Coffee Belt
note: Honduras is Central America's largest coffee producer, concentrated in the western highland departments of Copan, Lempira and Santa Barbara.

name: Uganda Central Robusta Belt
note: Uganda is Africa's leading coffee exporter, with robusta grown around the Lake Victoria crescent and arabica on Mount Elgon.

name: Peru Northern Highlands Coffee Belt
note: Peru is a leading organic and fair-trade arabica supplier, with production centered in Cajamarca, Amazonas and San Martin.

name: Guatemala Highland Coffee Belt
note: Guatemala produces prized high-altitude washed arabica across volcanic regions like Antigua, Huehuetenango and the Atitlan basin.

name: Soubre Cocoa District (Nawa)
note: The Nawa region around Soubre is described as the cocoa capital of Cote d'Ivoire, the single most productive cocoa zone on Earth.

name: San Pedro Cocoa Export Port
capacity: ~1.2 million t cocoa exports per year
note: San Pedro is the world's largest cocoa export port, handling roughly half of Ivorian cocoa shipments.

name: Ghana Western Region Cocoa Belt
note: Ghana's Western Region is the country's leading cocoa-producing area, supplying the bulk of the world's second-largest cocoa origin.

name: Cameroon Centre-South Cocoa Belt
note: Cameroon is the world's fourth-largest cocoa producer, with output concentrated in the Centre, South and Southwest regions.

name: Manabi Cocoa Belt (Arriba Nacional)
note: Manabi is a core zone for Ecuador's prized fine-flavor Arriba Nacional cocoa, the world's top fine-flavor origin.

name: Ondo State Cocoa Belt
note: Ondo State is Nigeria's largest cocoa-producing state, anchoring the country's position among the top global producers.

name: Sulawesi Cocoa Belt
note: Sulawesi accounts for most of Indonesia's cocoa output, making the country Asia's largest cocoa producer.

name: Port of Amsterdam Cocoa Hub
operator: Port of Amsterdam / bean storage operators
note: Amsterdam is the world's largest cocoa import and storage port, the pricing reference hub for European grinders.

name: Ribeirao Preto Sugarcane Cluster
capacity: tens of millions of t cane crushed per year
note: Ribeirao Preto is the dense heart of Sao Paulo's sugarcane industry, ringed by some of the world's largest cane mills.

name: Goias Sugarcane Belt
capacity: ~70 million t cane crushed per year
note: Goias is the fastest-growing center-west cane frontier and Brazil's second-largest sugarcane state after Sao Paulo.

name: Maharashtra Sugarcane Belt
note: Maharashtra rivals Uttar Pradesh as India's top sugar-producing state, dominated by cooperative mills in the Pune-Ahmednagar-Kolhapur region.

name: Central Thailand Sugarcane Belt
operator: Mitr Phol / Thai Roong Ruang mills
capacity: ~25 million t cane crushed per year
note: The central plains around Kanchanaburi and Suphan Buri host Thailand's largest cane mills, feeding the world's second-largest sugar exporter.

name: Queensland Coastal Sugarcane Belt
note: Queensland's wet tropical coast around Townsville and Mackay produces nearly all of Australia's sugar, most of it exported.

name: Florida Everglades Sugar Belt
note: The muck soils around Lake Okeechobee and Clewiston form the largest cane-sugar region in the United States.

name: Guangxi Sugarcane Belt
note: Guangxi produces roughly two-thirds of China's sugar, with cane fields concentrated around Chongzuo and Nanning.

name: Veracruz Sugarcane Belt
note: Veracruz is Mexico's leading sugarcane state, anchoring the country's role as a major sugar supplier to the United States.

name: Riau Palm Oil Belt
note: Riau is Indonesia's top palm-oil producing province and a core of the world's largest crude palm oil output.

name: North Sumatra Palm Oil District
note: North Sumatra around Medan hosts Indonesia's oldest oil-palm estates and major refining capacity.

name: Central Kalimantan Palm Oil Belt
note: Central Kalimantan is one of Indonesia's fastest-expanding oil-palm provinces on peat and mineral soils.

name: Peninsular Malaysia Palm Oil Belt (Johor/Pahang)
note: Johor and Pahang anchor Peninsular Malaysia's mature oil-palm estates and milling network.

name: Port Klang Palm Oil Export Terminal
note: Port Klang is a leading export gateway for Malaysian crude and refined palm oil.

name: Dumai Palm Oil Export Terminal
note: Dumai on Sumatra's east coast is Indonesia's principal crude palm oil export port.

name: Belawan Palm Oil Export Terminal
capacity: major North Sumatra palm export terminal
note: Belawan near Medan ships North Sumatra palm oil to global refiners.

name: Southern Thailand Palm Oil Belt (Krabi/Surat Thani)
note: Krabi and Surat Thani provinces form the core of Thailand's oil-palm belt, the world's third-largest producer.

name: Nigeria Southern Palm Oil Belt
note: Nigeria's southeastern states host smallholder groves and large estates as Africa's leading palm producer.

name: Colombia Eastern Plains Palm Oil Belt
note: Colombia's Llanos region makes it the largest palm-oil producer in the Americas.

name: Guatemala Palm Oil Belt
note: Guatemala's lowland estates make it Central America's top palm-oil exporter.

name: Papua New Guinea Palm Oil Belt (New Britain)
note: West New Britain estates anchor Papua New Guinea's export-oriented palm-oil sector.

name: Mississippi Delta Cotton Belt
note: The Mississippi Delta is a major irrigated upland-cotton region of the US Cotton Belt.

name: Georgia Coastal Plain Cotton Belt
note: Georgia's Coastal Plain is the top cotton-producing state in the US Southeast.

name: Gujarat Cotton Belt
note: Gujarat is India's largest cotton-producing state, centered on Bt hybrid upland cotton.

name: Maharashtra Vidarbha Cotton Belt
note: Vidarbha in Maharashtra is one of India's largest, mostly rain-fed cotton regions.

name: Pakistan Punjab Cotton Belt
note: Punjab around Multan is the heart of Pakistan's cotton economy and textile supply.

name: Pakistan Sindh Cotton Belt
note: Sindh's irrigated lower-Indus plain is Pakistan's second cotton region after Punjab.

name: Uzbekistan Cotton Belt (Fergana/Syr Darya)
note: Uzbekistan's irrigated Fergana and Syr Darya plains make it a leading Central Asian cotton producer.

name: Brazil Bahia (Oeste) Cotton Belt
note: Western Bahia's Cerrado is Brazil's second cotton frontier after Mato Grosso.

name: China Yangtze River Cotton Belt
note: The middle Yangtze plain is a historic Chinese cotton region outside Xinjiang.

name: Turkey Cukurova / Southeast Cotton Belt
note: Turkey's GAP-irrigated southeast and Cukurova plain supply its large textile industry.

name: Australia Namoi Valley Cotton Belt
note: The Namoi Valley in New South Wales is a core of Australia's high-yield irrigated cotton.

name: Benin Cotton Belt
note: Benin is West Africa's largest cotton producer, grown by northern smallholders.

name: Mali Cotton Belt (Sikasso)
note: The Sikasso region is the heart of Mali's smallholder cotton sector run via CMDT.

name: Northeast Thailand Rubber Belt (Isan)
note: Northeastern Thailand has become a major new smallholder rubber region beyond the traditional south.

name: South Sumatra Rubber Belt
note: South Sumatra around Palembang is Indonesia's leading natural-rubber producing province.

name: Jambi Rubber Belt
note: Jambi on Sumatra is a core smallholder natural-rubber province feeding Indonesian crumb-rubber plants.

name: Cote d'Ivoire Rubber Belt
note: Cote d'Ivoire is Africa's largest natural-rubber producer, concentrated in its humid south.

name: Peninsular Malaysia Rubber Belt
note: Peninsular Malaysia retains smallholder rubber estates feeding its large glove and tyre industry.

name: Kerala Rubber Belt
note: Kerala produces the bulk of India's natural rubber from dense smallholder plantations.

name: Vietnam Central Highlands Rubber Belt
note: The Central Highlands are a major expansion area for Vietnam's natural-rubber output.

name: China Yunnan (Xishuangbanna) Rubber Belt
note: Xishuangbanna in Yunnan is the core of China's limited domestic natural-rubber production.

name: China Hainan Rubber Belt
note: Hainan Island hosts state-farm rubber estates supplementing China's domestic supply.

name: Myanmar Mon/Tanintharyi Rubber Belt
note: Mon State and Tanintharyi form Myanmar's main natural-rubber producing region.

name: Cambodia Eastern Rubber Belt
note: Eastern Cambodia's provinces host fast-growing rubber estates and smallholdings.

name: Songkhla Rubber Export Port
note: Songkhla in southern Thailand is a principal export point for the world's largest rubber producer.
